accept: close --reason over 500 bytes either fails fast (with cap mentioned in error) or auto-truncates with stderr warning
payload.description: The 500-byte close-reason cap is documented in act help close but only surfaces on rejection after typing the command. Hit 5+ times in aac-website dogfood. Two acceptable resolutions: validate length when --reason is parsed and fail fast with a clear message naming the cap, OR auto-truncate with a stderr warning. Either is better than the rejection-after-the-fact pattern. Source: docs/aac-website-dogfood-debrief.md §1, §7.
payload.title: cli: act close --reason validates length upfront, not on rejection
